source_lists.title: Queer Horror
source_lists.desc:
All these teenagers are gonna come in for their queer horror, and it won't be there for them

(Not a complete master list, simply those I've seen. Cross referenced with this list.)
source_lists.owner: List by saint judas fonda PATRON

source_lists.title: Queer Horror
source_lists.desc:
A list of queer horror movies I’ve watched. There has to be an explicitly queer character(s). It cannot be homophobic / transphobic / misogynistic, etc. Definitely taking suggestions. This list also includes shorts and miniseries.
While I appreciate queer subtext in movies, there are plenty of lists out there which fulfill that need.
I am updating the list regularly. If you check the list view, each movie contains a note with a short description of why it's on the list. You can find the watchlist here.

Representation matters.

Ranking is according to my enjoyment which, changes regularly.

source_lists.title: Queer Horror

"Movies that have queer themes or ideas to chew on, interesting subtext, queer characters, or are just fun to look at through a queer lens. I'm only adding movies as I rate them, and the notes are a must-read."

Sex Demon (1975)

source_lists.title: Queer Horror
source_lists.desc:
A list that charts the evolution of queer horror from the early days of wistful, subtextual glances to explicit queer joy, liberation, and trans human/car hybrids. While Queer Horror (PDX) hostess & programmer Carla Rossi argues that all horror is queer, these are some of the MVPs in the saga of the monstrous queer on film.

Carla surveys subtext in her upcoming class Queer Horror: The Ifs, Ands, and Butts! For more info, click this link: siff.net/education.

source_lists.title: Queer horror
source_lists.desc:
Horror films with queer characters & queer storylines
I know there are plenty of lists like this but there always could be more

source_lists.title: Queer Horror
source_lists.desc:
Based off of the videos "Monsters in the Closet-A History of LGBT representation in horror cinema" and "Deep Cuts: Society and Queer Horror" both by James Somerton. Some of these movies aren't explicitly queer but the undertones, whether intentional or not, perfectly mirror the queer experience. 
If you're confused on why any of these movies are on the list cause "ThEy'Re NoT gAy!!!", then please watch the two videos mentioned.
